stories.title: FSD : la France dit officiellement non à la conduite 100 % autonome de Tesla
stories.summary: France has officially blocked Tesla’s Full Self-Driving (FSD) in supervised mode, despite approvals in the Netherlands, Lithuania, Estonia, Denmark and Belgium since April 2026. Transport Minister Philippe Tabarot cited safety concerns, including speeding and driver inattention, as reasons for the refusal. The government says Tesla must address these issues before any future authorization. No timeline for reconsideration has been announced.

stories.title: World Matchplay Darts: Updates, video, analysis, scores, commentary with Gerwyn Price, James Wade, Gian van Veen in action
stories.summary: The World Matchplay Darts 2026 continues Wednesday night with Cameron Menzies facing Ross Smith in a high-stakes match. Menzies stunned Luke Humphries Monday with a 170 checkout, while Smith opened with a 10-6 win over Kevin Doets, averaging 100.55 and hitting seven 180s. Smith leads in key stats with a 95.86 three-dart average and 0.34 180s per leg. The winner advances in the tournament running July 18-22.

stories.title: Lotto 6aus49 am Mittwoch: Das sind die Gewinnzahlen vom 22.7.2026
stories.summary: A 50-million-euro Lotto 6aus49 jackpot was drawn in Koblenz on Wednesday, 22 July 2026. The winning numbers were 3, 5, 10, 14, 25, 49 with a Superzahl of 3. The draw took place at 18:25. Players can now check their tickets and prepare for the next draw.

stories.title: Le PSG a fait une grosse offre à Ferran Torres
stories.summary: Paris Saint-Germain has made a significant bid for Spain’s World Cup-winning striker Ferran Torres, 26, following his decisive extra-time goal in the 2026 final. The move comes as Barcelona’s stalled contract talks leave the club’s future with Torres uncertain despite his contract running until 2027. PSG is now the frontrunner to sign the Spanish international, who is set to return to training after the tournament.

stories.title: 2 weeks after a Mississippi football player’s death, many questions loom
stories.summary: Nineteen-year-old Nolan Wells, a Mississippi community college football player, died 16 days after a Fourth of July boat trip to Horn Island. His body was recovered July 6 near the shoreline. An independent autopsy released Wednesday found no clear cause of death. Investigators continue reviewing the case as family and friends seek answers.

stories.title: ‘Terrifying’: Woman says landlord was hiding behind wall of Wexford holiday home
stories.summary: A woman and her family were left terrified after discovering a landlord hiding behind a wall in their Wexford holiday home last weekend. Therese, who booked the entire property via Booking.com for herself, her partner and two children, said the landlord assured her he wouldn’t be present when they returned from a family event. After leaving, she found the washing machine running and later noticed the man concealed behind a wall. Booking.com has suspended the property and launched an investigation into the incident.

stories.title: Pesticidi: richiamati riso Carnaroli, riso Roma integrale e kit pasta e fagioli
stories.summary: Il Ministero della Salute ha richiamato tre prodotti alimentari per eccesso di pesticidi: riso Carnaroli con tetrametrina, riso Roma integrale con clorpirifos e un kit pasta e fagioli per possibile glifosato nei fagioli. I richiami riguardano lotti specifici venduti in confezioni da 1 kg, con scadenza marzo 2027. Il clorpirifos, vietato in UE dal 2020, è tra le sostanze segnalate. I consumatori sono invitati a non consumare i prodotti richiamati e a restituirli ai punti vendita.

stories.title: Il faudra l'aval de l'employeur ou de France Travail avant de suivre une formation: le gouvernement veut (encore) réformer le CPF et cela suscite déjà des remous
stories.summary: The government plans to require employer or France Travail approval before using CPF training funds, under a reform announced by Labor Minister Jean-Pierre Farandou on July 16. The change aims to align training with job market realities and career goals, replacing the current personal use with professional validation. A brief automated questionnaire will assess each request for coherence. The reform faces opposition from unions like the CFDT.

stories.title: Sydney Sweeney kicks off ‘low-rise season’ in hip-hugging American Eagle jeans
stories.summary: Sydney Sweeney launched American Eagle’s low-rise jeans campaign on July 22, 2025, modeling wide-leg, boot-cut, skintight and baggy styles. The 28-year-old actress shared multiple Instagram posts and Stories featuring denim-on-denim looks, including a barefoot pose in a white tee and jeans. She also posed in camo pants and a cropped jean jacket, with some shots revealing her underwear. The campaign follows her July 2025 debut for the brand.

stories.title: Casemiro: Inter Miami being investigated for tampering after signing ex-Manchester United midfielder
stories.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for potential tampering after signing Brazil midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with a 2029 option. The league says it is reviewing the signing after the LA Galaxy held discovery rights to the free agent. Inter Miami confirmed the move, calling Casemiro a five-time Champions League winner. The investigation focuses on how Miami obtained his rights while the club prepares for his debut pending visa approval.

stories.title: Citizens Police Academy Registration Open Through July 31st
stories.summary: Registration for the Mountain Home Police Department’s Citizens Police Academy closes July 31. The free program, open to residents 21 and older, runs from August 10 through December 14 with monthly classes. Applicants must apply online at MountainHomeAR.gov and pass a background check. The academy offers hands-on exposure to patrol, investigations, K-9 units and dispatch.

stories.title: Man, 30s, dies after being struck by van in Co Kilkenny
stories.summary: A man in his 30s died after being struck by a van on the R693 near Arigan Bridge, Cooleshal, Freshford, Co Kilkenny at 9.30am on Wednesday, 22 July 2026. The pedestrian was pronounced dead at the scene before being taken to University Hospital Waterford for a post-mortem. Gardaí have appealed for witnesses or dash-cam footage from drivers travelling Freshford to Kilkenny between 9am and 9.30am. The road was temporarily closed but has since reopened.

stories.title: Mid-air mass brawl halts easyJet's Tenerife to Liverpool flight
stories.summary: A mid-air brawl involving around 10 passengers forced an easyJet plane flying from Tenerife to Liverpool to return to the Spanish island just 30 minutes after takeoff. The captain diverted due to safety concerns, and police met the flight upon landing. The aircraft later continued to Liverpool with its 186 passengers. EasyJet confirmed the disruptive behavior and stated it does not tolerate such incidents.

stories.title: How to watch White Sox vs. Rangers: TV channel and streaming options for July 22
stories.summary: The Chicago White Sox face the Texas Rangers at Globe Life Field in Arlington, Texas, at 8:05 p.m. ET on July 22. Anthony Kay starts for Chicago (53-47) against Texas’s Tyler Alexander (51-50). The game is available on RSN, CHSN, and streaming via Fubo. Both teams lead their divisions as the series heats up.

stories.title: Zendaya faces backlash for wearing ancient Iranian jewellery at Odyssey event
stories.summary: Zendaya faced backlash after wearing earrings made from ancient Iranian gold plaques at a London press event for the film Odyssey on May 14. The plaques, part of the 700BC Ziwiye hoard discovered in Iran, were mounted into jewelry by a Mayfair gallery in 2018. Archaeologists criticized the use of culturally significant artifacts as accessories, calling it an ethical violation. The debate highlights ongoing concerns over the display and commercialization of antiquities.

stories.title: Présidentielle 2027 : Jean-Luc Mélenchon tend une nouvelle fois la main aux Ecologistes en leur proposant un accord « global »
stories.summary: Jean-Luc Mélenchon proposed a broad electoral alliance with France’s Greens on July 22 for the 2027 presidential, legislative and 2028 regional votes. The offer, framed as a “global and squared” deal, builds on the 2022 Nupes program and targets the Greens as a party. Mélenchon repeated his commitment to a united left strategy under the banner of a “New Popular Alliance.” Negotiations on policy and candidacies are expected in coming months.

stories.title: RB Bragantino empata na Sul-Americana e decidirá vaga nas oitavas em casa
stories.summary: Sporting Cristal e Red Bull Bragantino empataram sem gols na partida de ida dos playoffs da Copa Sul-Americana, em Lima, no Peru, nesta quarta-feira, 22 de julho de 2026. O jogo de volta será no dia 29 de julho, em Bragança Paulista. O Bragantino, que controlou mais a posse no segundo tempo, buscará a classificação com a vantagem do empate. O vencedor enfrentará o Atlético Mineiro nas oitavas.

stories.title: Super Micro surges 15% on new order and margin disclosure after SpaceX announcement
stories.summary: Super Micro Computer shares surged 15% after the company raised its gross margin forecast to 15-17% for the June quarter, up from 8.2-8.4%, citing strong demand for AI servers. The update follows a June announcement by CEO Charles Liang about a new Gigawatt AI datacenter built for SpaceXAI. Rivals Dell and Hewlett Packard Enterprise also saw gains of 5% and 4% respectively. The company attributes the margin improvement to favorable customer and product mix.

stories.title: Elon Musk says Grok Imagine will make ‘historically accurate’ AI adaptation of Homer’s Odyssey
stories.summary: Elon Musk announced on July 22, 2026, that Grok Imagine will produce a full-length, AI-generated adaptation of Homer’s Odyssey by year’s end, claiming it will be historically accurate. The announcement follows Musk’s public criticism of Christopher Nolan’s recently released film, which grossed $264 million worldwide. Musk shared a three-minute AI-generated clip depicting Odysseus and Calypso. His campaign has targeted the casting choices in Nolan’s version.

stories.title: Shownieuws creëert verwarring over lot Greetje in Urk!: 'Doet echt niet meer mee'
stories.summary: Greetje Hakvoort blijft gewoon meedoen in realityserie Urk!, ondanks eerdere geruchten over haar vertrek. Presentator Teun Kapitein bevestigde dat ze nog steeds deel uitmaakt van het programma. Shownieuws suggereerde echter dat ze niet meer zou deelnemen, wat door de zender en Hakvoort zelf werd ontkracht. Het nieuwe seizoen van Urk! verschijnt binnenkort.

stories.title: Hosepipe ban in force for millions of households - is your area affected?
stories.summary: A hosepipe ban has taken effect for over 20 million households in southern and eastern England and parts of Wales, restricting use for watering gardens, washing vehicles and filling pools. The ban was introduced due to successive heatwaves and low rainfall, leaving water supplies under strain. This is the third major water restriction in the UK this year. Residents are advised to conserve water as the UK faces drier summers expected with climate change.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet saw its free cash flow turn negative for the first time in at least a decade, reaching -$5.9 billion in the latest quarter. This is due to soaring spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure, expected to reach $205 billion this year. The company's combined quarterly revenue rose 23% to $119.8 billion, but its stock fell 4% in after-hours trading. Alphabet's AI spending is driving a significant increase in capital expenditures, with $45 billion spent in the second quarter.

stories.title: Alphabet earnings takeaways: Q2 revenue beats, GOOGL stock sinks on 2026 capex hike
stories.summary: Google's Q2 revenue beats expectations, but its stock price drops due to a significant hike in planned 2026 capital expenditures, potentially reaching $205 billion. This increased spending is aimed at scaling the company's business and executing on its ambitious projects, such as the Gemini 4 AI model. Google is already dedicating resources to Gemini 4, planning to release new models almost every month. The company's focus on scaling its business and competing in the AI space is expected to drive growth in the long term.

stories.title: Elliot Page presenta la fidanzata Julia Shiplett: chi è la comica al suo fianco sul red carpet di The Odyssey
stories.summary: Elliot Page made a rare public appearance with his fiancée, Julia Shiplett, at The Odyssey premiere in Los Angeles on November 12. Shiplett, a Chicago-born comedian, writer, and actress, gained fame through her viral social media sketches and roles in Overcompensating and Crashing. The couple, who usually keep their relationship private, shared moments from the event online. Shiplett’s next project includes a new comedy series set for release next year.

stories.title: Inter Miami vs Chicago Fire live online: score, stats, goals & updates | MLS 2026
stories.summary: Inter Miami CF defeated Chicago Fire FC 3-2 in a Major League Soccer match. The final score was sealed in the 98th minute. The match saw a high-scoring second half with multiple attempts on goal from both teams. The win marks Inter Miami CF's latest victory in the 2026 season.

stories.title: Gruesome images in D4vd hearing are 'worst nightmare' for Celeste Rivas Hernandez's family
stories.summary: A preliminary hearing in Los Angeles revealed graphic crime scene images of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez, whose dismembered body was found last year inside a Tesla linked to singer David Anthony Burke. Prosecutors showed photos and testified Burke, 21, purchased a body bag, chainsaws, a shovel and an inflatable pool days after her death. Burke has pleaded not guilty to murder and sexual abuse charges. The hearing, not the trial, aims to establish enough evidence for the case to proceed.

stories.title: Preview: Inter Miami vs. Chicago Fire - prediction, team news, lineups
stories.summary: Inter Miami hosts Chicago Fire in MLS gameweek 16 at Nu Stadium on Wednesday. Miami, second in the Eastern Conference, had won four straight matches before the World Cup break, while Chicago entered on a three-game winning streak. The match is the midweek round’s biggest fixture. Miami’s recent wins included high-scoring games, but they kept just one clean sheet.

stories.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
stories.summary: A Lego Donkey Kong arcade machine has been created, priced at $200, featuring a basic fully functional game with a working joystick and button. The machine allows Mario to jump endless barrels, a mechanism that uses original barrels first released in 1989 for Lego's pirate sets. The set has reportedly won the approval of Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto. The machine's design is the work of Carl Merriam, a Lego designer known for his nostalgic sets.

stories.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
stories.summary: The Conservatives have formally asked HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on a £5 million gift from crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne. Farage claims the money was a personal gift with no conditions, while the Tories suggest it may have been compensation for political activity. HMRC has not confirmed if an inquiry has begun. Farage has previously stated the funds were for security and Brexit campaigning.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: China's Moonshot AI has allegedly stolen capabilities from top US AI model Anthropic's Fable AI, according to US President Donald Trump's Science and Technology adviser Michael Kratsios. The alleged theft occurred through a process called distillation, where a weaker AI model extracts answers from a stronger one. Moonshot AI gained access to restricted Nvidia servers to train its models, including the K3 model unveiled last week. The US government claims to have information on the alleged theft, which could have significant implications for the global AI landscape.

stories.title: Has Trump dragged the US into a forever war in Iran? | Mohamad Bazzi
stories.summary: A US-led war in Iran appears imminent as a ceasefire negotiated by former President Trump collapses, with reports suggesting a US military buildup in the region. The conflict could mark a return to the "forever wars" Trump had pledged to end during his presidency. The US has been at war in Afghanistan for nearly two decades and in Iraq since 2003. The collapse of the ceasefire raises concerns about the potential for a prolonged and devastating conflict.

stories.title: World Cup 2026: Gavi does not think Paredes should be suspended
stories.summary: Spain midfielder Gavi says Argentina’s Leandro Paredes should not face suspension for his role in a post-match altercation after the World Cup final. FIFA has opened a disciplinary review that could lead to suspensions or fines for players and coaches involved. Gavi, who was restrained during the fracas, told Spanish media he viewed the incident as part of football’s physical nature. Authorities are expected to decide on sanctions in the coming days.

stories.title: Opinion | Amnesty International UK vs. J.K. Rowling
stories.summary: Amnesty International UK released a report on July 8, 2026, accusing "gender critical" advocates of promoting anti-rights policies. The report targets figures like J.K. Rowling, framing their views as a growing threat to human rights. Amnesty’s stance has drawn criticism for allegedly straying from its traditional neutrality. The debate highlights ongoing tensions between free speech and gender rights advocacy.

stories.title: iPhone 18 Pro And iPhone 18 Pro Max Release Date: Apple’s Event Timeline Signals Exact Day
stories.summary: Apple will unveil the iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max on September 9, following its annual September keynote tradition. The launch will coincide with iOS 27, which is set to release after the public beta period. This marks Apple’s consistent pattern since 2012. Pre-orders are expected to begin shortly after the event.

stories.title: Chapecoense x Flamengo: onde assistir ao vivo, horário e escalações
stories.summary: Chapecoense faces Flamengo in a crucial Brasileirão match Wednesday at 9:30 p.m. Brasília time in Chapecó’s Arena Condá. The game, part of the 19th round, will air live on Globo, SporTV and Premiere. Chapecoense sits last with nine points after 18 matches, while Flamengo, seven points behind Palmeiras, returns motivated after four pre-season wins.

stories.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
stories.summary: Rising temperatures in Africa's Afar region are causing extreme heat stress in camels, with herders reporting blistered feet, watery eyes, and hair loss. In the past month, camel herder Ali Umer has lost eight camel calves due to the intense heat. The region is one of the hottest and driest places on Earth, where nomads lead camel caravans carrying salt through the desert. As temperatures continue to rise, camel herders are struggling to cope with the changing climate.

stories.title: Where to Find a Commonwealth Games 2026 Livestream in the U.S. to Watch the Event for Free
stories.summary: The 2026 Commonwealth Games will take place in Glasgow, Scotland, from July 23 to August 2, featuring over 7,000 athletes from more than 70 nations. North American viewers can watch the event for free on CBC's virtual private network, accessible with a VPN like ExpressVPN or NordVPN. The games will air live and as nightly highlights on various channels, including 7Plus in Australia and Channel 5 in the UK. Viewers can access the CBC's coverage by setting their VPN location to Canada.

stories.title: Falstart Roberta Lewandowskiego w debiucie w USA. Ta mina mówiła wszystko
stories.summary: Robert Lewandowski made his Chicago Fire debut on August 17 against Inter Miami, playing the full 90 minutes. His only shot on target was blocked in the 14th minute as Fire won 1-0. The match was moved from the previous week due to poor air quality. Chicago now faces LA Galaxy next on August 24.

stories.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
stories.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the agreement, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

stories.title: Inter Miami - Chicago Fire relacja na żywo
stories.summary: Inter Miami defeated Chicago Fire 2-1 in a Major League Soccer match on April 6. Miami’s Luis Suárez scored twice, including a late winner in the 87th minute. The victory keeps Inter Miami unbeaten in their last five league games. Next, Miami hosts Nashville SC on April 13.

stories.title: Amish Shah Wins Democratic Primary in Swing Arizona House District
stories.summary: Dr. Amish Shah, who beat the choice of the party’s House campaign arm, will face Jay Feely, a Republican, in a battleground district that covers parts of Phoenix.

stories.title: Republican Lawmaker Urges Charges Against Jack Smith
stories.summary: The criminal referral could provide a catalyst for a prosecution of Mr. Smith, who as special counsel became a top target of the president’s retribution campaign.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US and Saudi Arabia signed a landmark nuclear cooperation deal on April 26, allowing Riyadh to develop a civilian nuclear program with U.S. company involvement. The agreement includes a safeguards pact to prevent proliferation, though future uranium enrichment remains a concern. Experts warn this could set a precedent for nuclear expansion in a volatile region. The deal follows years of energy sector collaboration between the two nations.

stories.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has targeted Canada's dairy sector with a 50% tariff on $20 billion worth of Canadian goods imported to the US, set to take effect in August. The move is aimed at Canada's supply management system, which includes production quotas and set pricing for dairy, eggs, and poultry. Canadian politicians, including Quebec Premier Christine Fréchette, have indicated that dairy supply management is non-negotiable. The US trade action is likely to escalate tensions between the two countries.

stories.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Agents Amid Push for Private Security
stories.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

stories.title: Most sewage spills in England may be illegal, new research suggests
stories.summary: England’s water companies may have illegally spilled sewage during dry weather, with 86% of raw sewage discharges in 2021 occurring without heavy rain. The findings, based on analysis of 350 storm overflows serving over 30 million people, suggest most spills could violate regulations. The Environment Agency confirmed internal documents define heavy rain as beyond normal conditions, raising legal questions. Water companies face scrutiny as campaigners push for stricter enforcement and transparency.

stories.title: Houthis Claim Strikes on 2 Saudi Oil Tankers in Red Sea
stories.summary: The Saudi authorities had yet to confirm the hits, which would be the first attacks since the Iranian-backed Yemeni group announced a blockade on Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: Hope for thousands of MS patients as 'life-changing' drug now on NHS in England
stories.summary: Up to 5,000 people in England with multiple sclerosis will now have access to the life-changing drug fampridine, which boosts nerve signals to aid mobility. The medication, already available elsewhere in the UK, is the first to help MS patients walk. Clinical trials showed nearly four in 10 people experienced improvements in walking speed and duration. This development could significantly enhance the quality of life for those affected, enabling them to live independently.

stories.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
stories.summary: Drop-off fees at the UK's major airports have increased by a third on average since last summer, with two London airports, Gatwick and Stansted, charging the highest fees at £10. The fees are part of airports' business models and climate change objectives, with some airports offering free drop-offs. London City Airport now charges £8 for five minutes, equivalent to £1.60 per minute. The UK's airports are now more expensive than their EU counterparts, where none of the top 10 airports charge for drop-offs.

stories.title: McInnes glad of Rangers backing in appeal against ban
stories.summary: Rangers will appeal a four-match ban given to manager Derek McInnes for criticizing a late penalty awarded to Celtic in a May 13 match at Motherwell. The Scottish FA imposed the ban after McInnes called the decision "disgusting" while still at Hearts. Rangers argue the comments targeted the call, not officials. McInnes, who has managed for 19 years, says he’ll contest the ruling after a friendly win over St Etienne.

stories.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
stories.summary: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.

stories.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
stories.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

stories.title: Tears and sleepless nights as Marshall and Adlington get Scotland flag honour
stories.summary: Scottish athletes Marshall and Adlington have been chosen to carry the Scotland flag at the Commonwealth Games. Marshall, a 59-year-old bowler, and Adlington, a 39-year-old judoka, will lead the Scottish team into the opening ceremony. They have been keeping the news secret for weeks, revealing it on Wednesday. The flag will be returned to the Scottish team after the ceremony, but Marshall joked he might have to fight Adlington for first shot of it.

stories.title: Yeon Sang-ho Returns to ‘Parasyte’ Universe With ‘Tamiya,’ Starring Shioli Kutsuna, at Netflix
stories.summary: South Korean director Yeon Sang-ho is returning to the "Parasyte" universe with a new live-action series, "Parasyte: Tamiya," starring Shioli Kutsuna, set to stream on Netflix in 2027. This marks another collaboration between Netflix and Yeon, who also developed and co-wrote the script with Ryu Yong-jae.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
stories.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, a 23-year-old who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The move would see Liverpool beat out Arsenal and Bayern Munich for Barcola's signature. Talks are ongoing, but no official deal has been announced. A decision on the transfer is expected in the coming weeks.

stories.title: Shaken, Soaked and Seasick: What It’s Like to Survive ‘The Odyssey’ in 4DX
stories.summary: A 3-hour film experience in 4DX technology has been reported by multiple sources. The immersive format, available in select theaters, features seat-rumbling and water-spritzing effects. The technology is part of a growing trend in cinematic experiences. Audiences are now able to engage with movies in new and intense ways.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health committee report has found that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a result of individual willpower. The report recommends targeting ultra-processed foods, which are high in sugar and refined carbohydrates, as the primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The committee suggests that the current "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework is flawed and needs to be revised. Manufacturers are now expected to reformulate their products to reduce sugar content.

stories.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating in Homer’s ‘Odyssey’?
stories.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

offbeat.title: FSD : la France dit officiellement non à la conduite 100 % autonome de Tesla
offbeat.summary: France has officially blocked Tesla’s Full Self-Driving (FSD) in supervised mode, despite approvals in the Netherlands, Lithuania, Estonia, Denmark and Belgium since April 2026. Transport Minister Philippe Tabarot cited safety concerns, including speeding and driver inattention, as reasons for the refusal. The government says Tesla must address these issues before any future authorization. No timeline for reconsideration has been announced.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay Darts: Updates, video, analysis, scores, commentary with Gerwyn Price, James Wade, Gian van Veen in action
offbeat.summary: The World Matchplay Darts 2026 continues Wednesday night with Cameron Menzies facing Ross Smith in a high-stakes match. Menzies stunned Luke Humphries Monday with a 170 checkout, while Smith opened with a 10-6 win over Kevin Doets, averaging 100.55 and hitting seven 180s. Smith leads in key stats with a 95.86 three-dart average and 0.34 180s per leg. The winner advances in the tournament running July 18-22.

offbeat.title: Lotto 6aus49 am Mittwoch: Das sind die Gewinnzahlen vom 22.7.2026
offbeat.summary: A 50-million-euro Lotto 6aus49 jackpot was drawn in Koblenz on Wednesday, 22 July 2026. The winning numbers were 3, 5, 10, 14, 25, 49 with a Superzahl of 3. The draw took place at 18:25. Players can now check their tickets and prepare for the next draw.

offbeat.title: Le PSG a fait une grosse offre à Ferran Torres
offbeat.summary: Paris Saint-Germain has made a significant bid for Spain’s World Cup-winning striker Ferran Torres, 26, following his decisive extra-time goal in the 2026 final. The move comes as Barcelona’s stalled contract talks leave the club’s future with Torres uncertain despite his contract running until 2027. PSG is now the frontrunner to sign the Spanish international, who is set to return to training after the tournament.

offbeat.title: 2 weeks after a Mississippi football player’s death, many questions loom
offbeat.summary: Nineteen-year-old Nolan Wells, a Mississippi community college football player, died 16 days after a Fourth of July boat trip to Horn Island. His body was recovered July 6 near the shoreline. An independent autopsy released Wednesday found no clear cause of death. Investigators continue reviewing the case as family and friends seek answers.

offbeat.title: ‘Terrifying’: Woman says landlord was hiding behind wall of Wexford holiday home
offbeat.summary: A woman and her family were left terrified after discovering a landlord hiding behind a wall in their Wexford holiday home last weekend. Therese, who booked the entire property via Booking.com for herself, her partner and two children, said the landlord assured her he wouldn’t be present when they returned from a family event. After leaving, she found the washing machine running and later noticed the man concealed behind a wall. Booking.com has suspended the property and launched an investigation into the incident.

I consumatori sono invitati a non consumare i prodotti richiamati e a restituirli ai punti vendita.

The change aims to align training with job market realities and career goals, replacing the current personal use with professional validation. A brief automated questionnaire will assess each request for coherence. The reform faces opposition from unions like the CFDT.

offbeat.title: Sydney Sweeney kicks off ‘low-rise season’ in hip-hugging American Eagle jeans
offbeat.summary: Sydney Sweeney launched American Eagle’s low-rise jeans campaign on July 22, 2025, modeling wide-leg, boot-cut, skintight and baggy styles. The 28-year-old actress shared multiple Instagram posts and Stories featuring denim-on-denim looks, including a barefoot pose in a white tee and jeans. She also posed in camo pants and a cropped jean jacket, with some shots revealing her underwear. The campaign follows her July 2025 debut for the brand.

offbeat.title: Casemiro: Inter Miami being investigated for tampering after signing ex-Manchester United midfielder
offbeat.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for potential tampering after signing Brazil midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with a 2029 option. The league says it is reviewing the signing after the LA Galaxy held discovery rights to the free agent. Inter Miami confirmed the move, calling Casemiro a five-time Champions League winner. The investigation focuses on how Miami obtained his rights while the club prepares for his debut pending visa approval.

offbeat.title: Citizens Police Academy Registration Open Through July 31st
offbeat.summary: Registration for the Mountain Home Police Department’s Citizens Police Academy closes July 31. The free program, open to residents 21 and older, runs from August 10 through December 14 with monthly classes. Applicants must apply online at MountainHomeAR.gov and pass a background check. The academy offers hands-on exposure to patrol, investigations, K-9 units and dispatch.

offbeat.title: Man, 30s, dies after being struck by van in Co Kilkenny
offbeat.summary: A man in his 30s died after being struck by a van on the R693 near Arigan Bridge, Cooleshal, Freshford, Co Kilkenny at 9.30am on Wednesday, 22 July 2026. The pedestrian was pronounced dead at the scene before being taken to University Hospital Waterford for a post-mortem. Gardaí have appealed for witnesses or dash-cam footage from drivers travelling Freshford to Kilkenny between 9am and 9.30am. The road was temporarily closed but has since reopened.

offbeat.title: Mid-air mass brawl halts easyJet's Tenerife to Liverpool flight
offbeat.summary: A mid-air brawl involving around 10 passengers forced an easyJet plane flying from Tenerife to Liverpool to return to the Spanish island just 30 minutes after takeoff. The captain diverted due to safety concerns, and police met the flight upon landing. The aircraft later continued to Liverpool with its 186 passengers. EasyJet confirmed the disruptive behavior and stated it does not tolerate such incidents.

offbeat.title: How to watch White Sox vs. Rangers: TV channel and streaming options for July 22
offbeat.summary: The Chicago White Sox face the Texas Rangers at Globe Life Field in Arlington, Texas, at 8:05 p.m. ET on July 22. Anthony Kay starts for Chicago (53-47) against Texas’s Tyler Alexander (51-50). The game is available on RSN, CHSN, and streaming via Fubo. Both teams lead their divisions as the series heats up.

offbeat.title: Zendaya faces backlash for wearing ancient Iranian jewellery at Odyssey event
offbeat.summary: Zendaya faced backlash after wearing earrings made from ancient Iranian gold plaques at a London press event for the film Odyssey on May 14. The plaques, part of the 700BC Ziwiye hoard discovered in Iran, were mounted into jewelry by a Mayfair gallery in 2018. Archaeologists criticized the use of culturally significant artifacts as accessories, calling it an ethical violation. The debate highlights ongoing concerns over the display and commercialization of antiquities.

offbeat.title: Présidentielle 2027 : Jean-Luc Mélenchon tend une nouvelle fois la main aux Ecologistes en leur proposant un accord « global »
offbeat.summary: Jean-Luc Mélenchon proposed a broad electoral alliance with France’s Greens on July 22 for the 2027 presidential, legislative and 2028 regional votes. The offer, framed as a “global and squared” deal, builds on the 2022 Nupes program and targets the Greens as a party. Mélenchon repeated his commitment to a united left strategy under the banner of a “New Popular Alliance.” Negotiations on policy and candidacies are expected in coming months.

offbeat.title: RB Bragantino empata na Sul-Americana e decidirá vaga nas oitavas em casa
offbeat.summary: Sporting Cristal e Red Bull Bragantino empataram sem gols na partida de ida dos playoffs da Copa Sul-Americana, em Lima, no Peru, nesta quarta-feira, 22 de julho de 2026. O jogo de volta será no dia 29 de julho, em Bragança Paulista. O Bragantino, que controlou mais a posse no segundo tempo, buscará a classificação com a vantagem do empate. O vencedor enfrentará o Atlético Mineiro nas oitavas.

offbeat.title: Super Micro surges 15% on new order and margin disclosure after SpaceX announcement
offbeat.summary: Super Micro Computer shares surged 15% after the company raised its gross margin forecast to 15-17% for the June quarter, up from 8.2-8.4%, citing strong demand for AI servers. The update follows a June announcement by CEO Charles Liang about a new Gigawatt AI datacenter built for SpaceXAI. Rivals Dell and Hewlett Packard Enterprise also saw gains of 5% and 4% respectively. The company attributes the margin improvement to favorable customer and product mix.

offbeat.title: Elon Musk says Grok Imagine will make ‘historically accurate’ AI adaptation of Homer’s Odyssey
offbeat.summary: Elon Musk announced on July 22, 2026, that Grok Imagine will produce a full-length, AI-generated adaptation of Homer’s Odyssey by year’s end, claiming it will be historically accurate. The announcement follows Musk’s public criticism of Christopher Nolan’s recently released film, which grossed $264 million worldwide. Musk shared a three-minute AI-generated clip depicting Odysseus and Calypso. His campaign has targeted the casting choices in Nolan’s version.

offbeat.title: Shownieuws creëert verwarring over lot Greetje in Urk!: 'Doet echt niet meer mee'
offbeat.summary: Greetje Hakvoort blijft gewoon meedoen in realityserie Urk!, ondanks eerdere geruchten over haar vertrek. Presentator Teun Kapitein bevestigde dat ze nog steeds deel uitmaakt van het programma. Shownieuws suggereerde echter dat ze niet meer zou deelnemen, wat door de zender en Hakvoort zelf werd ontkracht. Het nieuwe seizoen van Urk! verschijnt binnenkort.

offbeat.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
offbeat.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet saw its free cash flow turn negative for the first time in at least a decade, reaching -$5.9 billion in the latest quarter. This is due to soaring spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure, expected to reach $205 billion this year. The company's combined quarterly revenue rose 23% to $119.8 billion, but its stock fell 4% in after-hours trading. Alphabet's AI spending is driving a significant increase in capital expenditures, with $45 billion spent in the second quarter.

offbeat.title: Alphabet earnings takeaways: Q2 revenue beats, GOOGL stock sinks on 2026 capex hike
offbeat.summary: Google's Q2 revenue beats expectations, but its stock price drops due to a significant hike in planned 2026 capital expenditures, potentially reaching $205 billion. This increased spending is aimed at scaling the company's business and executing on its ambitious projects, such as the Gemini 4 AI model. Google is already dedicating resources to Gemini 4, planning to release new models almost every month. The company's focus on scaling its business and competing in the AI space is expected to drive growth in the long term.

offbeat.title: Elliot Page presenta la fidanzata Julia Shiplett: chi è la comica al suo fianco sul red carpet di The Odyssey
offbeat.summary: Elliot Page made a rare public appearance with his fiancée, Julia Shiplett, at The Odyssey premiere in Los Angeles on November 12. Shiplett, a Chicago-born comedian, writer, and actress, gained fame through her viral social media sketches and roles in Overcompensating and Crashing. The couple, who usually keep their relationship private, shared moments from the event online. Shiplett’s next project includes a new comedy series set for release next year.

offbeat.title: Inter Miami vs Chicago Fire live online: score, stats, goals & updates | MLS 2026
offbeat.summary: Inter Miami CF defeated Chicago Fire FC 3-2 in a Major League Soccer match. The final score was sealed in the 98th minute. The match saw a high-scoring second half with multiple attempts on goal from both teams. The win marks Inter Miami CF's latest victory in the 2026 season.

offbeat.title: Gruesome images in D4vd hearing are 'worst nightmare' for Celeste Rivas Hernandez's family
offbeat.summary: A preliminary hearing in Los Angeles revealed graphic crime scene images of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez, whose dismembered body was found last year inside a Tesla linked to singer David Anthony Burke. Prosecutors showed photos and testified Burke, 21, purchased a body bag, chainsaws, a shovel and an inflatable pool days after her death. Burke has pleaded not guilty to murder and sexual abuse charges. The hearing, not the trial, aims to establish enough evidence for the case to proceed.

offbeat.title: Preview: Inter Miami vs. Chicago Fire - prediction, team news, lineups
offbeat.summary: Inter Miami hosts Chicago Fire in MLS gameweek 16 at Nu Stadium on Wednesday. Miami, second in the Eastern Conference, had won four straight matches before the World Cup break, while Chicago entered on a three-game winning streak. The match is the midweek round’s biggest fixture. Miami’s recent wins included high-scoring games, but they kept just one clean sheet.

offbeat.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
offbeat.summary: The Conservatives have formally asked HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on a £5 million gift from crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne. Farage claims the money was a personal gift with no conditions, while the Tories suggest it may have been compensation for political activity. HMRC has not confirmed if an inquiry has begun. Farage has previously stated the funds were for security and Brexit campaigning.

offbeat.title: Has Trump dragged the US into a forever war in Iran? | Mohamad Bazzi
offbeat.summary: A US-led war in Iran appears imminent as a ceasefire negotiated by former President Trump collapses, with reports suggesting a US military buildup in the region. The conflict could mark a return to the "forever wars" Trump had pledged to end during his presidency. The US has been at war in Afghanistan for nearly two decades and in Iraq since 2003. The collapse of the ceasefire raises concerns about the potential for a prolonged and devastating conflict.

offbeat.title: World Cup 2026: Gavi does not think Paredes should be suspended
offbeat.summary: Spain midfielder Gavi says Argentina’s Leandro Paredes should not face suspension for his role in a post-match altercation after the World Cup final. FIFA has opened a disciplinary review that could lead to suspensions or fines for players and coaches involved. Gavi, who was restrained during the fracas, told Spanish media he viewed the incident as part of football’s physical nature. Authorities are expected to decide on sanctions in the coming days.

offbeat.title: Opinion | Amnesty International UK vs. J.K. Rowling
offbeat.summary: Amnesty International UK released a report on July 8, 2026, accusing "gender critical" advocates of promoting anti-rights policies. The report targets figures like J.K. Rowling, framing their views as a growing threat to human rights. Amnesty’s stance has drawn criticism for allegedly straying from its traditional neutrality. The debate highlights ongoing tensions between free speech and gender rights advocacy.

offbeat.title: iPhone 18 Pro And iPhone 18 Pro Max Release Date: Apple’s Event Timeline Signals Exact Day
offbeat.summary: Apple will unveil the iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max on September 9, following its annual September keynote tradition. The launch will coincide with iOS 27, which is set to release after the public beta period. This marks Apple’s consistent pattern since 2012. Pre-orders are expected to begin shortly after the event.

offbeat.title: Chapecoense x Flamengo: onde assistir ao vivo, horário e escalações
offbeat.summary: Chapecoense faces Flamengo in a crucial Brasileirão match Wednesday at 9:30 p.m. Brasília time in Chapecó’s Arena Condá. The game, part of the 19th round, will air live on Globo, SporTV and Premiere. Chapecoense sits last with nine points after 18 matches, while Flamengo, seven points behind Palmeiras, returns motivated after four pre-season wins.

offbeat.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
offbeat.summary: Rising temperatures in Africa's Afar region are causing extreme heat stress in camels, with herders reporting blistered feet, watery eyes, and hair loss. In the past month, camel herder Ali Umer has lost eight camel calves due to the intense heat. The region is one of the hottest and driest places on Earth, where nomads lead camel caravans carrying salt through the desert. As temperatures continue to rise, camel herders are struggling to cope with the changing climate.

offbeat.title: Falstart Roberta Lewandowskiego w debiucie w USA. Ta mina mówiła wszystko
offbeat.summary: Robert Lewandowski made his Chicago Fire debut on August 17 against Inter Miami, playing the full 90 minutes. His only shot on target was blocked in the 14th minute as Fire won 1-0. The match was moved from the previous week due to poor air quality. Chicago now faces LA Galaxy next on August 24.

offbeat.title: Inter Miami - Chicago Fire relacja na żywo
offbeat.summary: Inter Miami defeated Chicago Fire 2-1 in a Major League Soccer match on April 6. Miami’s Luis Suárez scored twice, including a late winner in the 87th minute. The victory keeps Inter Miami unbeaten in their last five league games. Next, Miami hosts Nashville SC on April 13.

offbeat.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
offbeat.summary: The US and Saudi Arabia signed a landmark nuclear cooperation deal on April 26, allowing Riyadh to develop a civilian nuclear program with U.S. company involvement. The agreement includes a safeguards pact to prevent proliferation, though future uranium enrichment remains a concern. Experts warn this could set a precedent for nuclear expansion in a volatile region. The deal follows years of energy sector collaboration between the two nations.

offbeat.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
offbeat.summary: US President Donald Trump has targeted Canada's dairy sector with a 50% tariff on $20 billion worth of Canadian goods imported to the US, set to take effect in August. The move is aimed at Canada's supply management system, which includes production quotas and set pricing for dairy, eggs, and poultry. Canadian politicians, including Quebec Premier Christine Fréchette, have indicated that dairy supply management is non-negotiable. The US trade action is likely to escalate tensions between the two countries.

facts.title: Hosepipe ban in force for millions of households - is your area affected?
facts.summary: A hosepipe ban has taken effect for over 20 million households in southern and eastern England and parts of Wales, restricting use for watering gardens, washing vehicles and filling pools. The ban was introduced due to successive heatwaves and low rainfall, leaving water supplies under strain. This is the third major water restriction in the UK this year. Residents are advised to conserve water as the UK faces drier summers expected with climate change.

facts.title: Most sewage spills in England may be illegal, new research suggests
facts.summary: England’s water companies may have illegally spilled sewage during dry weather, with 86% of raw sewage discharges in 2021 occurring without heavy rain. The findings, based on analysis of 350 storm overflows serving over 30 million people, suggest most spills could violate regulations. The Environment Agency confirmed internal documents define heavy rain as beyond normal conditions, raising legal questions. Water companies face scrutiny as campaigners push for stricter enforcement and transparency.

facts.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating in Homer’s ‘Odyssey’?
facts.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

tech.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
tech.summary: A Lego Donkey Kong arcade machine has been created, priced at $200, featuring a basic fully functional game with a working joystick and button. The machine allows Mario to jump endless barrels, a mechanism that uses original barrels first released in 1989 for Lego's pirate sets. The set has reportedly won the approval of Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto. The machine's design is the work of Carl Merriam, a Lego designer known for his nostalgic sets.

crisis.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
crisis.summary: China's Moonshot AI has allegedly stolen capabilities from top US AI model Anthropic's Fable AI, according to US President Donald Trump's Science and Technology adviser Michael Kratsios. The alleged theft occurred through a process called distillation, where a weaker AI model extracts answers from a stronger one. Moonshot AI gained access to restricted Nvidia servers to train its models, including the K3 model unveiled last week. The US government claims to have information on the alleged theft, which could have significant implications for the global AI landscape.

crisis.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
crisis.summary: Drop-off fees at the UK's major airports have increased by a third on average since last summer, with two London airports, Gatwick and Stansted, charging the highest fees at £10. The fees are part of airports' business models and climate change objectives, with some airports offering free drop-offs. London City Airport now charges £8 for five minutes, equivalent to £1.60 per minute. The UK's airports are now more expensive than their EU counterparts, where none of the top 10 airports charge for drop-offs.

crisis.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
crisis.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

entertainment.title: Where to Find a Commonwealth Games 2026 Livestream in the U.S. to Watch the Event for Free
entertainment.summary: The 2026 Commonwealth Games will take place in Glasgow, Scotland, from July 23 to August 2, featuring over 7,000 athletes from more than 70 nations. North American viewers can watch the event for free on CBC's virtual private network, accessible with a VPN like ExpressVPN or NordVPN. The games will air live and as nightly highlights on various channels, including 7Plus in Australia and Channel 5 in the UK. Viewers can access the CBC's coverage by setting their VPN location to Canada.

entertainment.title: Yeon Sang-ho Returns to ‘Parasyte’ Universe With ‘Tamiya,’ Starring Shioli Kutsuna, at Netflix
entertainment.summary: South Korean director Yeon Sang-ho is returning to the "Parasyte" universe with a new live-action series, "Parasyte: Tamiya," starring Shioli Kutsuna, set to stream on Netflix in 2027. This marks another collaboration between Netflix and Yeon, who also developed and co-wrote the script with Ryu Yong-jae.

entertainment.title: Shaken, Soaked and Seasick: What It’s Like to Survive ‘The Odyssey’ in 4DX
entertainment.summary: A 3-hour film experience in 4DX technology has been reported by multiple sources. The immersive format, available in select theaters, features seat-rumbling and water-spritzing effects. The technology is part of a growing trend in cinematic experiences. Audiences are now able to engage with movies in new and intense ways.

breaking.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
breaking.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the agreement, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

breaking.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Agents Amid Push for Private Security
breaking.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

breaking.title: Houthis Claim Strikes on 2 Saudi Oil Tankers in Red Sea
breaking.summary: The Saudi authorities had yet to confirm the hits, which would be the first attacks since the Iranian-backed Yemeni group announced a blockade on Saudi Arabia.

politics.title: Amish Shah Wins Democratic Primary in Swing Arizona House District
politics.summary: Dr. Amish Shah, who beat the choice of the party’s House campaign arm, will face Jay Feely, a Republican, in a battleground district that covers parts of Phoenix.

politics.title: Republican Lawmaker Urges Charges Against Jack Smith
politics.summary: The criminal referral could provide a catalyst for a prosecution of Mr. Smith, who as special counsel became a top target of the president’s retribution campaign.

politics.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
politics.summary: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.

health.title: Hope for thousands of MS patients as 'life-changing' drug now on NHS in England
health.summary: Up to 5,000 people in England with multiple sclerosis will now have access to the life-changing drug fampridine, which boosts nerve signals to aid mobility. The medication, already available elsewhere in the UK, is the first to help MS patients walk. Clinical trials showed nearly four in 10 people experienced improvements in walking speed and duration. This development could significantly enhance the quality of life for those affected, enabling them to live independently.

health.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
health.summary: A British health committee report has found that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a result of individual willpower. The report recommends targeting ultra-processed foods, which are high in sugar and refined carbohydrates, as the primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The committee suggests that the current "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework is flawed and needs to be revised. Manufacturers are now expected to reformulate their products to reduce sugar content.

sports.title: McInnes glad of Rangers backing in appeal against ban
sports.summary: Rangers will appeal a four-match ban given to manager Derek McInnes for criticizing a late penalty awarded to Celtic in a May 13 match at Motherwell. The Scottish FA imposed the ban after McInnes called the decision "disgusting" while still at Hearts. Rangers argue the comments targeted the call, not officials. McInnes, who has managed for 19 years, says he’ll contest the ruling after a friendly win over St Etienne.

sports.title: Tears and sleepless nights as Marshall and Adlington get Scotland flag honour
sports.summary: Scottish athletes Marshall and Adlington have been chosen to carry the Scotland flag at the Commonwealth Games. Marshall, a 59-year-old bowler, and Adlington, a 39-year-old judoka, will lead the Scottish team into the opening ceremony. They have been keeping the news secret for weeks, revealing it on Wednesday. The flag will be returned to the Scottish team after the ceremony, but Marshall joked he might have to fight Adlington for first shot of it.

sports.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
sports.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, a 23-year-old who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The move would see Liverpool beat out Arsenal and Bayern Munich for Barcola's signature. Talks are ongoing, but no official deal has been announced. A decision on the transfer is expected in the coming weeks.
